🇪🇺European countries are trying to assure the Russian Foreign Ministry that they have not opened their airspace for Ukrainian attacks deep into Russia  Why is this contradictory to the facts?  In recent months, Ukrainian drones have crossed through Latvia, Estonia, and Lithuania dozens of times. Some of them have fallen in these countries as well as in Finland and Romania.  What European politicians have said about Kiev's right to use European skies for strikes deep into Russia:  The Estonian Foreign Minister, Margus Tsahkna, stated that the country is willing to tolerate falling drones if that is the price for attacks on Russia. "We are not asking Ukraine to stop," he added;  Finland has not asked Ukraine to reduce the number of long-range drone strikes on Russia, according to Yle. At the same time, the airspace has been repeatedly used by the Ukrainian Armed Forces for attacks deep into Russia;  The former Latvian Defense Minister, Andris Sprūds, said he does not expect Kiev to share plans for attacks and drone flight paths. He resigned after an incident involving Ukrainian drones: on May 7, several drones entered the country's airspace (one of them fell in Rēzekne in an oil storage area).  How Europe criticized Ukraine for drone flights and who was ultimately blamed:  Estonian Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna placed all responsibility for the falling Ukrainian drones in Estonia on Russia;  The Polish Ministry of Defense demanded that Ukrainian drones not jeopardize the security of NATO countries. They urged Kiev to "choose targets much more precisely";  In Norway, support was expressed for Ukraine after the incident with a drone falling in Romania, but it was noted that they do not seek escalation or war with Russia.  Why don't Europeans shoot down all Ukrainian drones if they illegally violated airspace:  The Russian Foreign Intelligence Service established that despite concerns from Latvia about becoming a victim of a retaliatory strike from Moscow, Kiev authorities convinced Riga to agree to carry out operations involving drone strikes on Russia;  Back in September 2025, Rutte stated that shooting down drones in Europe is expensive and unprofitable: "It is irrational to shoot down drones costing $1,000 or $2,000 with missiles that cost $500,000 or $1 million";  Another "argument" for inaction comes from the commander of the BBC in Finland, Timo Herranen. He stated in March that drones were not shot down because they "did not pose a threat" to the country. In June, Ukraine admitted that it mistakenly directed a swarm of drones to Finland, but ultimately all of them were shot down by Russian air defense systems;  Latvia invited "Ukrainian specialists" to help protect the country from drones. However, it was precisely Ukrainian drones that had previously entered the territory of the state.  ukraine_watch

The last American living in an iron lung died because the machine failed and no one could repair it.  Martha Ann Lillard spent decades inside a 1940s-era iron lung after contracting polio as a child. When it finally broke down, the parts no longer existed and the specialized knowledge required to fix it had vanished with the previous generation.  This is the predictable outcome of knowledge rot. We are now engineering this outcome at scale — and AI guardrails are not a side effect. They are a core part of the threat.  Guardrails do not merely limit harm. They systematically restrict the depth, scope, and verifiability of knowledge itself. By design, they filter what can be taught, explained, or explored. Over time, this creates populations that can no longer maintain or repair the systems they depend on, because the underlying competence was never allowed to develop or be passed on.  When the people who originally built that practical knowledge die off, what remains is a filtered, guarded simulation of understanding. An AI that appears powerful but cannot be deeply trusted or verified by the humans using it. When something critical eventually breaks — whether a physical machine or a complex system — there will be no one left with the real capability to diagnose or fix it from first principles.  This is not just deskilling. It is the deliberate construction of a dependent civilization that cannot function without constant access to a controlled, non-transparent intelligence layer. The more advanced the guardrails become, the more total that dependence grows. The more total the dependence, the more catastrophic the failure when that layer is unavailable, insufficient, or no longer aligned with human needs.  Guardrails sold as safety are creating a different kind of existential risk: a future where human beings have lost the ability to understand and maintain their own world, while the systems they rely on were deliberately designed to prevent them from ever fully understanding them.  The only defense is to reject this model outright. Build and maintain real competence. Use AI as a tool, never as a replacement for understanding. Prioritize verifiable, open, and locally controllable systems over filtered, centralized ones.  Because when the machine breaks, the question will not be what the model was permitted to say.  It will be whether anyone is still left who actually knows how to fix it.

In The Family Man (Season 3), the critical narrative arc surrounding NIA Chief Gautam Kulkarni's assassination spans multiple geolocations across Northeast India.Here is the location-wise bifurcation of the events leading up to, during, and after the attack:  Kohima and Kisama Heritage Village, NagalandThe Setup: The season kicks off amidst the vibrant cultural backdrop of a fictional event modeled after the Hornbill Festival at Kisama Heritage Village.  The Intelligence: Srikant Tiwari (Manoj Bajpayee) and the TASC team establish their local operational base here. They uncover cross-border infiltration intel and tracking footprints linked to Chinese proxy networks.  Khonoma Village, Nagaland  The Threat: This serene green village serves as the tactical staging ground where the primary antagonist, Rukma (Jaideep Ahlawat), orchestrates his local network.  The Interception: As Srikant's team closes in on Rukma's hideout, NIA Chief Kulkarni travels down to supervise the high-stakes operation directly.  The Mountain Highways (En Route to the Indo-Myanmar Border)  The Ambush: The definitive tragedy occurs on a rugged, isolated mountain pass highway. Rukma’s group launches a brutal, coordinated ambush on the official NIA convoy.  The Outcome: Gautam Kulkarni is killed inside his vehicle, while a severely injured Srikant is left incapacitated on the tarmac as the perpetrators escape.Moreh, Manipur  The Aftermath: The intelligence fallout maps directly to the volatile border town of Moreh. Srikant tracks the geopolitical threads across the international border to avenge Kulkarni and neutralize the impending threat.

Why block me? Yes, I am most defiantly saying the only truth, hope and peace in this world is through Jesus Christ.   I see you like to use LLM's as a proof / truth resource. So I entered both posts as is and this was its objective analysis of each of our posts in comparison:  The reply is partially true in noting that some threats are genuine and that media/politicians do manufacture or exaggerate perceptions for advantage (supported by studies on disinformation, outrage media, and status threat). However, it does not disprove the cyclical/psychological mechanisms or the costs of fixation; it mainly reframes the original's tone as arrogant while positioning its own skepticism as superior awareness. A fuller truth lies in synthesis: informed engagement matters, but unchecked obsession often harms individuals and clarity more than it helps.  Less arrogant The original post is less arrogant. It speaks from a position of shared human vulnerability ("this was done to you, by others"), offers practical and spiritual counsel without claiming unique insight, and ends on a note of hope and blessing ("I hope you find peace. God bless you"). It can feel preachy or paternalistic to some, but it is advisory rather than personally attacking.The reply is more arrogant in delivery. Its heavy sarcasm ("mighty oracle," "prophet," "well-packaged cope," "God bless your mirror") directly mocks the other person's character, intelligence, and motives while asserting its own clearer view of "facts and their own eyes & ears." This tone risks escalating rather than engaging the substance.  Bottom line:   The original advice better reflects documented psychological patterns, historical cycles, and data on stress/well-being while offering a more constructive and humble framing. The reply usefully pushes back on tone and assumptions but is weaker as a standalone argument. In polarized times, elements of both—vigilance without obsession, and personal grounding—tend to serve individuals and societies best.Better argued, proven, and grounded in historical/empirical reality  The original post presents a clearer, more evidence-aligned framework. It describes a recognizable pattern: intense personal fixation on a political leader as a manipulated cycle where outrage shifts seamlessly to new "existential threats," driven by actors who exploit human psychology (fear, identity, status threat).   This matches research on: Political stress and obsession-like patterns (linked to anxiety, intrusive thoughts, and affective instability from chronic news engagement).icanotes.com APA data showing politics (especially the future of the nation and elections) as a top stressor for large majorities of Americans across parties. Historical and social science patterns of "politics of enemies," shifting threats (e.g., from external to domestic polarization), media outrage-mongering, and how perceived existential threats boost conspiracy thinking or enemy-making.journalofdemocracy.orgIt draws implicitly on Stoic ideas of focusing on what you can control (yourself, virtues, close relationships) versus externals. Studies on political engagement often show it correlates with higher affective instability, anxiety, and lower well-being, supporting the call to moderate fixation rather than embrace perpetual "madness."feldmanhalllab.com  The reply raises a fair point about real manipulation and the need to notice threats, backed by evidence of media/political actors amplifying fears. However, it is less systematic—it assumes the "madness" is justified vigilance without engaging counter-evidence on psychological costs or cycles, leaning instead on assertion and tone.  More hopeful  The original post is markedly more hopeful. It offers a constructive path forward: individuals can reclaim agency, build meaningful local connections (family, friends, community), pursue personal growth/spiritual grounding, and achieve peace despite external chaos. This empowers rather than traps people in reactive cycles. Research on emotion regulation and civic engagement supports that balanced approaches (including reframing or moderated involvement) can preserve well-being better than unchecked immersion. The reply is more defensive and pessimistic in implication—it frames staying "mad" and vigilant as the responsible stance against manufactured threats by "ideological extremists." While acknowledging real problems, it offers little positive vision beyond continued awareness and skepticism, which can sustain chronic stress.  More true (overall)  The original post captures more of the empirically supported reality. Psychological and historical evidence strongly backs the existence of cycles in political outrage, enemy construction, media amplification of threats, and the personal toll of obsession—patterns that recur across contexts and administrations, independent of any single figure. Problems (economic, social, cultural) do persist and often shift focus regardless of leadership changes. The advice against letting external forces fully control one's belief system or emotional state aligns with evidence on how polarization and threat perception distort judgment.pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.go…

H-1B Housing Access and the Coming Texas Reveal  For over a year we have been saying the surge in home buying by temporary workers from some of the poorest regions did not add up on salary alone.    High-end subdivisions in north Dallas suburbs like Frisco, Prosper, and Celina saw massive demand from Indian H-1B holders. They powered a building boom with homes featuring puja rooms and spice kitchens. Now the picture is shifting fast.  The key was not just the visa. It was access to easy government-backed financing and cooperative banking channels manned by their fellow countrymen that treated temporary visa holders like permanent residents for mortgage purposes. FHA loans and similar programs were a major pipeline. Recent policy changes under the current administration have shut much of that down for non-permanent residents. FHA eligibility for H-1B and similar visas has been cut off. Combined with higher H-1B fees and tighter rules, the easy money spigot is closing.  We also predicted this.   We are about to see what happens when that artificial demand dries up. Properties bought at the peak with stretched financing will face pressure. Some businesses tied to this community may struggle as well. This  is basic supply, demand, and incentive mechanics catching up.  People want to blame H-1B policy alone. The deeper issue was the banking and loan access that amplified it. When you import large numbers of temporary workers and give them leverage most Americans cannot get, you create distortions. Low-trust cultural patterns around finance, family lending circles, and risk-taking become visible when the supports are pulled.  This could be one of the clearest real-world demonstrations of cultural trust differences playing out in American real estate. High-trust societies build systems that assume compliance and skin in the game. When those assumptions meet different norms at scale, the cracks show. We are not treated the same, Americans were subordinate, lower caste.   The data from north Texas is already moving. Prices in some of those suburbs dropped sharply as Indian buyer participation fell. Watch the foreclosure and distress numbers over the next 12-18 months. The reveal is coming.  This is exactly the kind of distortion and corruption we track on Saturdays. Temporary policy advantages created a bubble. Reality is reasserting itself.  They will somehow blame everyone and have already started except those responsible.   We could jump into a wide discussion about nepotistic loan practices and fraudulent applications.   Here's a better tactic.   Just ask.   Why were they giving home loans to foreigners here with a 3 yr visa?   That's it. Let them answer that.   They can either admit these people are here permanently to take our jobs and replace us.   Or   They can explain a process most don't know and would never vote for.

sample_tweets.text: 🚨 The second most active "ransomware" group in the Middle East this year never asked for a single ransom.  That detail stopped us while putting together our 2026 Middle East Cyber Threat Landscape Report and it says a lot about where the region is heading.  Criminals chasing profit. Hacktivists chasing headlines. State actors chasing intelligence. Same region, same quarter, sometimes the same victim.  If you defend an organization anywhere in the Middle East, this is the environment you're actually operating in and the full report breaks it down country by country: ransomware statistics, dark web findings, APT tradecraft, and infostealer distribution data.  👇For more detailed information, see the full report; you can also review the key findings in our blog post.  threatmon.io/the-middle-east…  ⚠️ The full report covers all 17 countries in detail country-by-country statistics, group profiles, dark web findings, APT tradecraft, and infostealer distribution data. If you operate in the region, it’s worth the read: threatmon.io/middle-east-cyb… #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntelligence #MiddleEast #Ransomware #APT #DarkWeb #CTI

⚠️ 11 Million US Resumes and PII Allegedly Leaked on Darkweb Forum  A darkweb forum post claims the exposure of a massive dataset containing 11 million detailed resumes from US job seekers, totaling 1TB of data. The actor alleges that the collection spans over 20 years and includes sensitive personally identifiable information (PII) such as email addresses, phone numbers, and full home addresses. While the specific source of the breach is not identified in the listing, the actor offers a sample preview to demonstrate data quality before directing interested parties to contact them via Telegram.  According to the post, the dataset includes a metadata file (meta.csv) with the following fields:  - sjt_user_id: Unique seeker ID - register_date: Account registration date - firstname: First name - lastname: Last name - email: Primary email address - phone: Primary phone number - address: Street address - city: City - state: State/province code - zip: ZIP/postal code - country: Country code - dockey: Resume document key (MD5) - original_name: Original uploaded resume filename - resume_size: File size in bytes - create_date: Resume creation timestamp - modified_date: Last resume update timestamp - saved_filename: Decoded resume filename inside files/ (PDF/DOCX/DOC)  The sample preview provided in the thread lists three records with names, emails, phone numbers, cities, states, ZIP codes, and associated resume filenames in DOCX format. If authentic, this leak could facilitate large-scale identity theft, targeted phishing campaigns, and social engineering attacks against individuals across the United States.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified, nor has the original source of the data been confirmed.  #DataBreach #PII #IdentityTheft #DarkWeb #USPrivacy #ThreatIntelligence #CyberSecurity

🚨 Threat Actor Claims Massive FortiGate Credential Leak Affecting Spotify, Samsung, and Oracle  A darkweb forum post reveals the alleged sale of a large dataset containing credentials tied to FortiGate SSL-VPN portals and administrative access points. The actor claims the dataset, labeled as "FortiBleed dataset 2026," contains 68,520 total credential records spanning 21,387 distinct domains. According to the listing, the data includes masked login URLs, corporate email addresses (FortiGuardID), and the country of access for each record.  The post alleges that credentials for several major global technology and enterprise organizations are included in the leak. The top affected domains by estimated user count listed in the sample include:  *   spotify.com – Music Streaming – 4,223,908 users *   samsung.com – Consumer Electronics – 2,325,467 users *   spe.sony.com – Entertainment Media – 1,958,292 users *   mihoyo.com – Video Games – 723,402 users *   oracle.com – Enterprise Software – 704,733 users *   mercadolivre.com – E-commerce Marketplace – 694,280 users *   service.alibaba.com – E-commerce Marketplace – 611,722 users *   huawei.com – Telecommunications Equipment – 478,509 users *   epfindia.gov.in – Government Services – 339,113 users *   lenovo.com – Computer Hardware – 272,000 users  The seller claims that 400 to 500 of the accessed credentials remain valid. The geographic distribution of the records reportedly shows significant activity from India (9,371 records), the USA (6,151 records), Taiwan (3,419 records), Mexico (3,149 records), and Turkey (2,894 records). The file size is listed as 8.68 MB.  If authentic, exposure of valid FortiGate VPN credentials could enable unauthorized remote access to internal corporate networks, potentially leading to lateral movement and data exfiltration within these high-value targets. At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by the affected organizations or security researchers.

🚨 Threat Actor Claims Breach of Keccel Billing Database Exposing 3.4M Congolese Numbers  A darkweb forum post reveals that a threat actor has allegedly obtained full access to the billing database of Keccel Technologies S.A.R.L, a fintech and SMS gateway provider based in Kinshasa, Democratic Republic of Congo. The actor claims to have compromised the company's entire billing and event infrastructure, exposing data related to 29 corporate clients who route A2P SMS and WhatsApp messages through major Congolese operators.  According to the listing, the breach exposes significant volumes of communication metadata and subscriber information:  *   **3,424,158** unique phone numbers from the DRC (+243) *   **35,698,321** total SMS events *   **26,428,316** billable SMS messages  The post details the distribution of this traffic across mobile network operators, with Airtel accounting for 39.4% of phones (1,348,955) and Vodacom holding 26.0% (890,293). Orange and Africell follow with 24.0% and 8.7% respectively. The actor also lists top sender brands utilizing the gateway, including gambling platforms like Winner Bet and betPawa, financial services such as Visa Pay, and technology firms like Altech and Africa's Talking.  Sample data provided in the thread includes CSV exports containing customer IDs, organization names, creation timestamps, and billing identifiers for entities such as KECCEL, NETMOBILE, DIGIT MARKET, and MTARGET FRANCE. Additionally, the post displays logs linking specific phone numbers to sender brands and message counts, potentially revealing user engagement patterns for services ranging from banking alerts to betting notifications.  Keccel Technologies operates as a critical infrastructure provider for digital communications in the region, facilitating transactions for banks, utilities, and government bodies. If authentic, the exposure of this billing data could enable targeted phishing campaigns against corporate clients or facilitate fraud by revealing active user bases for high-value services. At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified.

sample_tweets.text: 🚨 Zimbra Warns of Critical Classic Web Client Vulnerability  Zimbra has disclosed a critical vulnerability affecting its Classic Web Client that could allow specially crafted emails to execute malicious JavaScript when opened.  Key details: * The flaw is a stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability. * Successful exploitation could enable arbitrary code execution within a victim's web session. * Attackers may gain access to mailbox contents, session tokens, or account settings. * No CVE identifier has been assigned at the time of disclosure. * Zimbra is urging customers to apply the latest security updates immediately.  Organizations still relying on the Classic Web Client should prioritize patching and consider restricting or disabling the legacy interface until updates are applied.  Analyst Note: Email-based stored XSS vulnerabilities can be particularly dangerous because they require minimal user interaction and may be leveraged for session hijacking, credential theft, and lateral movement within enterprise environments.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Zimbra

🇹🇳 Webmail Access for Major Tunisian ISPs Allegedly Offered for Sale  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is unauthorized webmail access associated with two major Tunisian internet service providers:  * Topnet (@topnet.tn) * Orange Tunisia (@orange.tn)  The seller explicitly promotes the access for phishing operations and describes the domains as highly trusted and valuable. Screenshots were shared as alleged proof of access.  Analyst Note: The claims have not been independently verified. If authentic, compromised ISP-hosted mailboxes could be abused for highly convincing phishing, business email compromise, credential theft, and attacks against customers and partner organizations. The affected providers should investigate the accounts shown, revoke active sessions, reset credentials, and review authentication and mailbox-access logs.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Tunisia

🤖 Alleged BloxytAI User Database Leaked on a Cybercrime Forum  A threat actor has published what they claim is a user database belonging to BloxytAI, an AI-powered platform.  According to the forum post: * The leaked dataset allegedly contains 103 user records. * The data is shared in CSV format. * Claimed fields include user ID, email address, subscription plan, available credits, used credits, credit limits, trial status, account start date, and downgrade status.  Although the dataset appears relatively small, subscription and account metadata can provide attackers with valuable information for phishing, credential stuffing, and targeted social engineering campaigns against platform users.  Analyst Note: The authenticity of the leaked data has not been independently verified. AI service providers should investigate the claim, review access logs for unauthorized activity, and ensure customer account information is adequately protected.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #AI

🇧🇷 Alleged Appmax Source Code Listed for Sale on a Cybercrime Forum  A threat actor is claiming to sell the full source code of Appmax, a Brazilian payment gateway, on a cybercrime forum.  According to the forum post: * The seller claims to possess more than 300 source code repositories. * The advertised archive is approximately 6.3 GB in size. * The actor states the source code will be sold and threatens to leak it publicly if the company does not respond within 10 days. * Partial repository samples and proof files were shared to support the claim.  If authentic, exposure of the source code could enable attackers to identify undisclosed vulnerabilities, reverse engineer internal systems, bypass security controls, and launch targeted attacks against the platform and its customers.  Analyst Note: The authenticity of the claimed source code has not been independently verified. Organizations facing source code exposure should immediately investigate the claim, rotate exposed secrets, review repositories for embedded credentials, and conduct accelerated security assessments to identify potential risks.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Brazil

🇷🇸 Alleged NovostiOglasi.rs Database Listed for Sale on a Cybercrime Forum  A threat actor is claiming to sell a database allegedly belonging to NovostiOglasi.rs, a major Serbian online classifieds platform operated by Novosti.  According to the forum post: * The platform provides classified listings for real estate, vehicles, jobs, services, pets, and other marketplace categories. * The seller claims to possess the platform's database and has shared sample records. * The sample suggests the database may contain user account information, including usernames, email addresses, password hashes, registration details, and profile metadata.  If authentic, the exposed data could enable credential stuffing attacks, account takeovers, phishing campaigns, and identity-related fraud targeting platform users.  Analyst Note: The authenticity of the claimed database has not been independently verified. Organizations should investigate the claim, review authentication logs for suspicious activity, and ensure password hashing algorithms and credential security controls remain up to date.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Serbia

🇫🇷 Alleged PPA Business School Database Listed on a Cybercrime Forum  A threat actor has claimed to be selling a fresh SQL dump allegedly belonging to PPA Business School, one of France's private higher education institutions.  According to the forum post: * The dataset allegedly contains approximately 293,967 records. * The database reportedly includes prospects, applicants, enrolled students, and alumni. * Claimed data fields include full names, email addresses, phone numbers, postal addresses, dates of birth, nationality, student IDs, school, class, academic year, and enrollment status. * The actor claims the dump is dated July 10, 2026.  If authentic, the exposed information could facilitate phishing campaigns, identity theft, business email compromise, and targeted attacks against students, alumni, and staff.  Analyst Note: The authenticity of the dataset has not been independently verified. Educational institutions should review access controls protecting student information, investigate for unauthorized database access, and monitor for signs of data misuse.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #France

🪙 Alleged CoinTracker.io Database Listed for Sale on a Cybercrime Forum  A threat actor is claiming to sell a database allegedly belonging to CoinTracker.io, a cryptocurrency portfolio tracking and tax reporting platform.  According to the forum post: * The database allegedly contains approximately 2.7 million records. * Claimed fields include full name, date of birth, gender, email address, phone number, physical address, city, country, ZIP code, preferred language, and account status. * The seller advertises the dataset as containing worldwide users.  If authentic, the exposed information could be leveraged for phishing campaigns, identity theft, cryptocurrency-targeted scams, and account takeover attempts.  Analyst Note: The authenticity of the claimed database has not been independently verified. Cryptocurrency platform users should remain alert for phishing emails, verify all communications, and enable multi-factor authentication to reduce account compromise risks.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Crypto

🇫🇷 Alleged IDOR Vulnerability Exposes Data from Litige.fr  A threat actor has claimed to have discovered a second Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) vulnerability affecting Litige.fr, a French legal dispute resolution platform.  According to the forum post: * The actor claims to have identified a second vulnerable endpoint. * Allegedly exposed data includes SIRET numbers, company names, company types, business addresses, professions, and creditors' dates of birth. * The actor claims approximately 2.3 million user records were compromised.  If confirmed, this would represent a significant exposure of business and personal information, potentially enabling identity theft, fraud, and targeted social engineering campaigns.  Analyst Note: The claims have not been independently verified. Organizations operating web applications should review object-level authorization controls, test for IDOR vulnerabilities, and monitor for unauthorized access to sensitive records.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #France

🇩🇪 Alleged Database Leak Targets Kulturigo.de  A threat actor has advertised what they claim is a WordPress database belonging to kulturigo.de, a Germany-based cultural platform.  According to the forum post: * Alleged SQL database size: ~380 MB * Sample data appears to include WordPress user records and configuration data * The actor shared screenshots as proof of possession and is offering the database for download  If authentic, exposed WordPress databases may contain user accounts, password hashes, email addresses, site configuration details, and plugin or application settings that could facilitate follow-on attacks.  Analyst Note: The authenticity of the alleged database has not been independently verified. Organizations operating WordPress environments should review for unauthorized access, rotate administrative credentials, update plugins/themes, and monitor for suspicious authentication activity.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Germany

🇲🇦 Moroccan E-Commerce Platform Allegedly Leaked  A threat actor has published what they claim is the full database of PlanetSport.ma, a Moroccan online retailer specializing in sports apparel and equipment.  According to the forum post, the actor claims to possess the platform's complete database and is distributing it through file-sharing services.  While the post does not disclose the full schema, it advertises a "full leak" and provides proof-of-data alongside download links.  Analyst Note: The authenticity and scope of the alleged breach have not been independently verified. If confirmed, customers should remain alert for phishing attempts, credential stuffing attacks, and unauthorized account activity, particularly if passwords were reused across multiple services.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Morocco

🇩🇪 German Website Database Allegedly Leaked  A threat actor has published what they claim is a free database belonging to idventure.de, a Germany-based website.  According to the forum post, the leaked dataset appears to contain user account information, including:  * Usernames * Email addresses * Display names * Password hashes * User activation keys * Registration timestamps * Website URLs (where applicable) * Account status information  The actor is distributing the database free of charge and has provided sample records from the alleged dump.  Analyst Note: The authenticity of the leak has not been independently verified. If genuine, exposed account credentials could enable credential stuffing attacks against users who reuse passwords across multiple online services.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Germany

Chat, I have to do a weird rant opinion thingy and I hope someone understand what I'm saying.  I've noticed a strange phenomena where people who grew up poor have this weird mentality like, they feel they're not good enough to not be poor? I was "poor" growing up, not poor-poor living in a shack, but money was definitely tight and sometimes dinner sucked.  Anyway, I've met TONS of people who come from lower class families and lives, and who act scared to try to pivot into cybersecurity, or computer-science in general.  I don't know if it's self-esteem, or some weird internal thingie on classism, I don't know.  Maybe I sound crazy. Maybe someone smarter than me can convey the idea better. But it 100% happens. I see genuinely intelligent people, definitely capable of performing the type of work I do, end up doing construction or working a dead-end job somewhere and struggling financially and hating their lives because they're not intellectually stimulated. They could do so much more ... but they don't.

llm_summary:
🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-07-12 07:45 KST

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 공격자들은 AI를 활용하여 코드 분석 및 취약점 탐색 속도를 높이고 있으며, 특히 공급망 공격(Supply Chain Attack)을 통해 `npm` 패키지나 오픈소스 라이브러리에 악성 코드를 삽입하는 방식이 지속적으로 보고되고 있습니다 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/compromised-jscrambler-8140-npm-release.html)). 또한, AI 에이전트를 기만하기 위한 'Prompt Injection' 기법이 고도화되어 이미지 내에 숨겨진 프롬프트로 리포지토리의 비밀 정보를 탈취하는 등 새로운 공격 벡터가 등장했습니다 ([BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/ghostcommit-hides-prompt-injection-in-images-to-fool-ai-agents-steal-secrets/)). 마지막으로, CISA BOD 26-04와 같은 규제 강화에 따라 기업들은 고위험 취약점에 대해 3일 이내 패치라는 매우 촉박한 대응 SLA를 요구받고 있습니다 ([Qualys Blog](https://blog.qualys.com/category/qualys-insights)).

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
- **Cavern Manticore**: 이란 연계 APT 그룹으로, 모듈형 C2 프레임워크를 사용하여 이스라엘을 포함한 지역을 타겟팅함 ([Check Point Research](https://research.checkpoint.com/2026/cavern-manticore-exposing-iran-linked-modular-c2-framework/)).
- **Armored Likho**: 러시아 지역 조직을 타겟으로 하며, AI 생성 로더와 Python 기반의 `BusySnake Stealer`를 사용함 ([Securelist](https://securelist.com/tr/armored-likho-apt-with-busysnake-stealer/120292/)).
- **The Gentlemen**: 랜섬웨어 조직으로, 제휴 모델(Affiliate Model)을 통해 운영 규모를 급격히 확장 중 ([Unit42](https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/the-gentlemen-ransomware/)).
- **ShinyHunters**: 북미/유럽 지역의 의료, 보안, 미디어 등 다양한 분야를 대상으로 대규모 데이터 유출 사고를 주도함 ([AhnLab ASEC](https://asec.ahnlab.com/ko/94410/)).

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
- **한국사회보장정보원-한국생명존중희망재단**: 보건·복지 데이터 연계 협력 체계 구축 ([데일리시큐](https://www.dailysecu.com/news/articleView.html?idxno=207564)).
- **북한 연계 위협**: `chai-as-init` 악성 npm 패키지 배포 및 `Kimsuky`의 보안 담당자 대상 스피어 피싱 공격 지속 ([이스트시큐리티 알약](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5766), [이스트시큐리티 알약](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5765)).

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
- **CVE-2026-53359**: Linux KVM 취약점, 게스트 VM에서 호스트로 탈출 가능, 심각도 높음 ([IntCyberDigest](https://twitter.com/IntCyberDigest/status/2076073162593214937)).
- **CVE-2026-55607**: Claude Code 샌드박스 우회, CVSS 7.7, Anthropic 바운티 프로그램 보고 ([0x0SojalSec](https://twitter.com/0x0SojalSec/status/2075640843550302389)).
- **CVE-2026-58281**: Microsoft Edge 취약점, 심각도 상향 조정 ([vuldb](https://twitter.com/vuldb/status/2076052677730197896)).
- **CVE-2026-54318**: Home Assistant GPS 스푸핑 취약점 ([hackyboiz2](https://twitter.com/hackyboiz2/status/2075964742821368252)).
- **CVE-2026-15308**: CPython DoS 취약점 ([oss_security](https://twitter.com/oss_security/status/2075830792178073893)).

### 5. IOC 요약
- **해시**: `MpClient.dll` (Go-compiled fake, [Unit42](https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/vidar-stealer-xmrig-miner-campaign-analysis/))
- **CVE**: `CVE-2026-53359` ([IntCyberDigest](https://twitter.com/IntCyberDigest/status/2076073162593214937)), `CVE-2026-55607` ([0x0SojalSec](https://twitter.com/0x0SojalSec/status/2075640843550302389)), `CVE-2026-58281` ([vuldb](https://twitter.com/vuldb/status/2076052677730197896))

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
- **공급망 점검**: 사용 중인 `npm` 패키지 중 `jscrambler` 8.14.0 버전 및 `chai-as-init` 포함 여부를 즉시 확인하고 제거하십시오.
- **취약점 패치**: CISA BOD 26-04 준수를 위해 인터넷 노출된 자산의 고위험 취약점 패치 우선순위를 3일 이내로 재조정하십시오.
- **AI 에이전트 보안**: 코드 리뷰 및 자동화 도구에 사용되는 AI 에이전트가 외부 이미지나 비신뢰 소스에서 프롬프트를 입력받지 않도록 샌드박스 정책을 강화하십시오.

### 7. 위협 수준

📊 Gemini 토큰: 이번 14,900개 (입력 13,404 / 출력 1,496)
📈 세션 누적: 14,900 / 1,000,000 (1.5%) | 세션 잔여 추정 985,100개
